Title: Transcriptome analysis reveals a comprehensive insect resistance response mechanism in cotton to infestation by the phloem feeding insect Bemisia tabaci (whitefly)

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes whitefly resistance; silencing increases adult whitefly and egg/pupa densities and suppresses JA/ET-associated defense gene expression.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; qRT-PCR; VIGS silencing followed by whitefly infestation and insect counts; downstream gene expression analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated after whitefly infestation; functionally important especially in resistant HR cultivar
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bemisia tabaci infestation for 12, 24, 48 h; VIGS plants challenged for 2 weeks
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhMPK3 was induced after whitefly infestation in cotton. 2. VIGS of GhMPK3 markedly increased whitefly adults and eggs/pupae, demonstrating a positive role in resistance. 3. Silencing GhMPK3 reduced expression of WRKY40, ERF1, JAZ1 and AOC4, linking MPK3 to MPK-WRKY-JA and ET defense pathways.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering whitefly resistance by maintaining or enhancing MPK3 signaling; could be stacked with downstream WRKY/JA/ET components.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although a kinase rather than enzyme of nutrient metabolism, it was the principal functionally validated resistance gene connected to hormone-associated defense metabolism in this study.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TRV-based VIGS system; endogenous promoter otherwise NA

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to whitefly resistance, but its silencing caused only a moderate increase in whitefly counts.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; VIGS silencing followed by whitefly infestation; RT-PCR/qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhMPK6 was induced by whitefly infestation. 2. VIGS of GhMPK6 caused only a moderate increase in whitefly density compared with stronger effects for GhMPK3. 3. The paper places GhMPK6 in MAPK-mediated regulation of WRKY and hormone pathways during resistance.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary candidate for resistance engineering, likely best combined with GhMPK3-centered signaling components.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional effect was weaker and cultivar-dependent knockdown efficiency was variable.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TRV-based VIGS system; endogenous promoter otherwise NA

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing did not significantly alter whitefly abundance, indicating no major validated effect on resistance under tested conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; VIGS silencing followed by whitefly infestation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhHSP20 was selected as a candidate from transcriptome data. 2. VIGS of GhHSP20 did not significantly change whitefly densities. 3. Thus the study did not validate it as a major determinant of defense-associated metabolism.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low immediate value based on this study alone.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was subjected to functional testing in Results, although negative/weak phenotype.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TRV-based VIGS system; endogenous promoter otherwise NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes unstable allene oxide intermediate to form 12-oxo-phytodienoic acid in the jasmonate pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor of jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defense signaling contributing to whitefly resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression was highly induced after 48 h infestation; reduced after GhMPK3 silencing, consistent with positive contribution to JA-mediated defense.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling; qRT-PCR for MPK3-dependent expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced during infestation; reduced in GhMPK3-silenced HR plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC4 is a JA biosynthetic gene responsive to whitefly infestation. 2. Its transcript increased at late infestation stages. 3. GhMPK3 silencing reduced AOC4 expression, linking MAPK signaling to JA biosynthesis in resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful JA-pathway enzyme target for strengthening whitefly resistance, potentially alongside MPK3/WRKY40.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a candidate gene analogous to rice brown planthopper resistance gene AOC4.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts fatty acid hydroperoxide to allene oxide in jasmonate biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defense signaling contributing to whitefly resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AOS transcripts were highly expressed after 48 h infestation, implicating activation of JA biosynthesis during whitefly response.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOS was induced during whitefly infestation, especially at late stages. 2. This supports activation of the JA biosynthetic pathway in cotton defense. 3. The gene is part of the metabolite synthesis cohort highlighted by transcriptome analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate JA-pathway enzyme for resistance engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct genetic manipulation of AOS performed in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts S-adenosyl-L-methionine to ACC, the direct precursor of ethylene.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defense signaling contributing to whitefly resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Displayed distinct expression patterns between resistant and susceptible cultivars, supporting ET pathway involvement in whitefly response.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Distinct in HR and ZS after infestation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACS1, an ethylene biosynthesis gene, responded differentially to whitefly infestation. 2. Its behavior supported involvement of ET signaling in cotton defense. 3. The paper integrates ACS1 with ERF1 and MPK3-linked signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune ET-mediated resistance, though pleiotropy should be considered.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct functional perturbation of ACS1 performed.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones to produce dihydroflavonols in the flavonoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: potential defensive secondary metabolites in insect/pathogen responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: F3H expression declined after infestation, especially dramatically in susceptible ZS at 48 h, suggesting reduced flavonoid biosynthetic capacity during susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High before infestation in HR, moderately decreased after infestation; strongly reduced in ZS after infestation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differential between HR and ZS, especially at 48 h
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F3H was among flavonoid-pathway genes dynamically regulated during whitefly infestation. 2. qRT-PCR confirmed strong repression in susceptible ZS after 48 h. 3. The data support a link between altered flavonoid metabolism and resistance differences.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential metabolic marker or engineering target for preserving flavonoid defenses under whitefly attack.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study infers metabolite pathway involvement from transcript behavior; flavonoid levels were not directly quantified here.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form chalcone, the entry metabolite of flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: potential defensive secondary metabolites in insect/pathogen responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CHS expression varied dramatically in ZS and was confirmed to be down-regulated in later-stage whitefly infestation, consistent with suppression of flavonoid pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Dramatic variation in ZS after infestation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Down-regulated during later stage of infestation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS was one of the flavonoid-related genes altered by whitefly infestation. 2. qRT-PCR confirmed its expression change in susceptible ZS. 3. The paper links repression of CHS/F3H/DFR/PAL to altered secondary defense metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for maintaining flavonoid biosynthesis under pest attack.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct functional test on whitefly phenotype.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: defense-related hormone signaling and secondary metabolism-associated responses
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: JA-related genes; ET-related genes; connected hub genes in co-expression module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Associated with activation of cotton defense network; expression positively linked with resistance and MPK3 signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Hub gene in co-expression network likely regulating cotton defenses to whitefly infestation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression in resistant HR than susceptible ZS; dramatically reduced after GhMPK3 silencing, implicating it as a central regulator of resistance-associated metabolism/signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; WGCNA co-expression network; qRT-PCR; expression analysis in GhMPK3-silenced plants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated after whitefly infestation; higher in HR than ZS at 12 h
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by infestation and dependent on GhMPK3
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY40 was identified as a hub gene in the co-expression network of whitefly-responsive DEGs. 2. Its expression was elevated in both cultivars but more strongly in resistant HR. 3. GhMPK3 silencing sharply reduced WRKY40 expression, placing it downstream of MAPK signaling in whitefly resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate regulator for engineering broad whitefly resistance, especially with MPK3-centered pathway activation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper infers regulatory importance from network position and MPK3-dependence rather than direct promoter-binding assays.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Marker and likely regulator of ET pathway activation in resistance response
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Whitefly infestation; GhMPK3 signaling; ethylene pathway
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of ET-associated defense response
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ERF1 expression was elevated after infestation and higher in HR than ZS at 12 h; it was suppressed by GhMPK3 silencing, linking ET signaling to resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; expression analysis after GhMPK3 VIGS
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Abundant during initial infestation stage; higher in HR than ZS at 12 h
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERF1 was among the most abundant transcription factors early in susceptible plants and showed higher expression in resistant HR than ZS at 12 h. 2. It was reduced after GhMPK3 silencing. 3. These data support ET-pathway regulation as part of cotton whitefly defense.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful ET-pathway regulatory target for resistance engineering, likely in combination with MPK3 or JA pathway components.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct gain/loss-of-function of ERF1 itself was performed.
